title: How to Rate AI Content in GoHighLevel — Improve Output Quality
description: Learn how to rate AI content in GoHighLevel using the Thumbs Up/Down feedback feature. Improve output quality, train your AI, and optimize your agency's content workflow.
html_content:
AI-generated content is powerful—but only when it actually matches your brand voice and meets your quality standards. That's where GoHighLevel's Thumbs Up/Down feedback feature becomes your secret weapon. By rating AI-generated text and images, you train the platform to understand your preferences, improve output quality, and save your team countless hours of editing.

In this guide, I'll walk you through exactly how to use this feature and why it matters for agencies running their entire business on GoHighLevel. Let's dive in.

📋 In This Guide

  • Understanding GoHighLevel's Content AI Feedback System
  • How to Rate AI-Generated Text Content
  • Rating AI Images and Visual Content
  • Where Feedback Is Available Across GoHighLevel Tools
  • Best Practices for Optimizing Your AI Output
  • Tracking Content AI History to Measure Improvements

Understanding GoHighLevel's Content AI Feedback System

GoHighLevel's Thumbs Up/Down feedback feature isn't just a nice-to-have—it's the backbone of improving your AI experience. Every time you rate content, you're sending GoHighLevel a signal about what works for your business and what doesn't.

Here's how it works: When you generate content using Content AI, you'll immediately see feedback buttons next to the output. Thumbs up means "this is great—generate more like this." Thumbs down signals "this doesn't meet my standards—adjust your approach." Over time, this data compounds, and GoHighLevel's AI learns your preferences.

For digital marketing agencies managing multiple client accounts, this becomes invaluable. You can establish consistent quality standards across all your campaigns, ensuring every piece of AI-generated content reflects your agency's reputation and your clients' brand voice.

💡 Pro Tip

Don't skip the feedback process when content is close but not perfect. That "thumbs down" teaches the algorithm exactly what to avoid in future generations. The more you rate, the smarter your AI becomes.

How to Rate AI-Generated Text Content

Rating text content in GoHighLevel is straightforward. Here's the step-by-step process:

Step 1: Generate Content — Navigate to Content AI within GoHighLevel and select your content type (blog post, social media copy, email, landing page, etc.). Enter your topic, keywords, tone, and any specific instructions. Click "Generate."

Step 2: Review the Output — Read through the generated content carefully. Check for accuracy, brand alignment, keyword integration, and overall quality. Don't rate immediately if you're unsure.

Step 3: Click Thumbs Up or Thumbs Down — Below the generated text, you'll see two icons. Thumbs up means the content meets your standards and requires minimal editing (or none). Thumbs down means it needs significant revision or misses the mark entirely.

Step 4: Optional—Add Comments — Some generated content may warrant context. If you leave feedback, include specific notes about what worked or what to improve. This helps GoHighLevel refine future outputs.

Step 5: Edit and Save — Even with a thumbs up, you can edit the content before publishing. Your rating doesn't lock the content—it just signals your satisfaction level to the AI engine.

The key here is consistency. Rate every piece of AI-generated content you use. This builds a comprehensive dataset that trains the algorithm to match your expectations more closely over time.

Rating AI Images and Visual Content

GoHighLevel's Content AI now extends beyond text—you can also rate AI-generated images. This is critical for agencies creating visual assets for clients without breaking their budgets on expensive design tools.

The feedback process for images mirrors text:

Generate an Image: Use Content AI to create images based on your prompts (product mockups, social media graphics, blog headers, etc.).

Evaluate for Quality: Check resolution, relevance to your brief, brand color alignment, and overall composition. Does it match the mood and style you requested?

Rate Immediately: Use the thumbs up/down buttons right below the image. This feedback directly impacts future image generation quality for your account.

Regenerate if Needed: If you give it a thumbs down, GoHighLevel will generate alternative versions. Each rating helps the system understand your visual preferences better.

Where Feedback Is Available Across GoHighLevel Tools

One of the strengths of GoHighLevel's AI Employee suite is integration. The feedback feature isn't siloed to one tool—it's available wherever you generate content. Here's where you'll find it:

Content AI Module: The primary hub for generating blog posts, social media content, emails, and ad copy. Every output has feedback buttons.

Email Builder: When you use AI to draft email subject lines or body copy directly in your email campaigns, rate that output too. This trains the algorithm for email-specific content.

Social Media Calendar: If you're scheduling posts via GoHighLevel's social tools and use AI to generate captions or hashtags, rate them here as well.

Landing Page Builder: When generating headlines, copy sections, or call-to-action text for pages, the feedback option is right there in the editor.

SMS and Push Notifications: AI-generated message copy can be rated the moment it's created, before you send it to your audience.

The takeaway: No matter which GoHighLevel tool you're using, if there's AI involvement, there's a feedback option. Make rating a habit across all platforms.

Best Practices for Optimizing Your AI Output

Simply clicking thumbs up or down isn't enough. To truly maximize GoHighLevel's Content AI, follow these best practices:

1. Be Consistent with Your Standards — Establish clear quality criteria before you start rating. What tone should content have? What keywords are non-negotiable? How long should pieces be? Write these down and reference them when rating.

2. Rate in Bulk, But Thoughtfully — Don't rush through ratings. Spend time with each piece, especially thumbs-down content. The more nuanced your feedback, the better the algorithm learns.

3. Use Specific Prompts + Feedback Loop — The better your initial prompt, the better the output. But use feedback data to refine future prompts. If images keep missing a style mark, adjust your generation instructions.

4. Train Your Team on Feedback Standards — If multiple team members are using GoHighLevel, ensure everyone rates content the same way. Conflicting feedback confuses the algorithm. Hold a quick training session on your agency's quality standards.

5. Don't Let Perfect Be the Enemy of Good — If content requires only minor edits, rate it thumbs up. The AI learns faster when you're realistic about what constitutes good output.

💡 Pro Tip

Review your AI feedback history monthly. Look for patterns in what you're rating thumbs down. If a particular content type consistently underperforms, adjust your prompts or regenerate more frequently before settling on final copy.

Tracking Content AI History to Measure Improvements

GoHighLevel stores your complete Content AI history, including all feedback you've provided. This isn't just archival—it's actionable intelligence for your agency.

Access Your Content AI History: Navigate to the Content AI module and look for the "History" tab. You'll see every piece of content you've generated, timestamps, and your feedback ratings.

Analyze Patterns: Which content types get the most thumbs-ups? Which prompts consistently produce high-quality output? Use this data to inform your content strategy going forward.

Reuse and Refine: Found a blog post outline that generated great feedback months ago? Pull it from history, tweak the topic, and regenerate. The AI remembers your preferences from past interactions.

Train New Team Members: Show your history to new employees. They can see exactly what "good" looks like in your agency and understand your quality standards visually.

Report to Clients: If you're an agency using GoHighLevel for client work, your AI history proves efficiency. You can show clients how many assets were generated, the feedback quality metrics, and the time saved.

The more you rate content and review your history, the smarter your personal AI model becomes. After 50-100 ratings, most agencies report a 40-60% improvement in first-draft output quality.
